description_long: This lighthouse features a tall, octagonal tower with a rust-colored upper section and a white, cylindrical lower base. The upper portion is constructed from what appears to be weathered metal or concrete, with several small, square windows distributed around its circumference. The lower section is painted white and includes a prominent arched double-door entrance, as well as a few small windows. At the top, a metal railing encircles the flat roof, suggesting a gallery balcony, but there is no visible lantern room or light beacon. The structure is robust and utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, and is likely designed for durability in harsh coastal or urban environments. The overall appearance is functional, with a slight industrial influence.
description: A tall, octagonal lighthouse with a rust-colored upper tower, white cylindrical base, and a gallery balcony at the top. It features small windows and a double-door entrance, with a functional, industrial look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched double-door entrance and two small windows on the upper tower.
description_view_1: Side view with a single arched window at the base and two upper windows visible, no entrance door.
description_view_2: Rear view with a single arched window at the base and two upper windows, similar to view 1 but rotated further.
description_view_3: Side view with only one arched window at the base and two upper windows, no entrance door visible.
